avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Snowfall and drifting deposits will continue to form unstable windslab in wind sheltered locations on South-West to North-West aspects above 900 metres, with moderately bonded accumulations below this altitude. Moderately bonded windslab will also be present on North and South aspects, due to both existing and newly developing accumulations. Fragile cornices will continue to form. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snowfall overnight to glen level, easing by mid-morning. Strong North-East winds, easing to fresh later. Freezing level around 450 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Overnight snowfall above 700m, lighter during the day falling to 450 metres. Strong Easterly winds. Freezing level around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Snowfall and drifting have formed moderate to weakly bonded windslab in wind sheltered locations on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres. Localised areas of moderately bonded windslab are present on these aspects, below 900 metres and above 700 metres. Fragile cornices are forming. Wind exposed slopes are scoured and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Areas of windslab will be found in wind sheltered locations mainly on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres. Although not particularly extensive or deep, these areas will not be well bonded. On other aspects the snowpack will be firm, icy and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Turning colder with the freezing dropping to 400 metres. After a few overnight snow showers it will generally be dry. Easterly winds will back to the North-East and ease.
Observed Weather Influences: Light snow overnight and during the morning petered out to leaver a generally dry afternoon. Strong winds were from the South-East. The freezing level was around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The old snowpack has frozen in the cool conditions and is hard, stable and icy. However, areas of fresh windslab were being deposited overnight and during the morning on mainly  sheltered West to North aspects above 900 metres. Although not particularly extensive or deep, these deposits are poorly bonded. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Areas of fresh windslab will continue to build on South through West to North-West aspects above 800 meters in the East of the region. (Fannaich\'s and Fisherfield mountains). These deposits are expected to be localised and easily avoidable. Areas affected will be gully tops and corrie rims. Mountains in the West around Glen Torridon will remain unaffected and the snowpack here will remain well bonded, icy and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force Easterly winds easing and backing to the North-East. Light, intermittent showers expected. The freezing level is forecast to lower to around 400 meters.
Observed Weather Influences: Gale force East-South-East winds. Light showers falling as snow above around 500 meters. The freezing level is 600 meters.
ObservedSnowStability: Small accumulations of fresh windslab are present on South-West to North-West aspects above 800 meters in the East of the region (Fannaich\'s and Fisherfield mountains). These deposits are localised and easily avoidable. Areas affected are gully tops and corrie rims. Mountains in the West of the region remain unaffected and the snowpack here remains well bonded and stable. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Unstable windslab will continue to build on South-West through West to North-West aspects above 900 metres. Accumulations are not expected to be extensive but will affect wind-sheltered terrain such as coire rims and gully tops. Cornices will build above the same aspects. Elsewhere the snowpack will be firm, icy and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Light overnight snowfall, drier thereafter. The freezing level  will fluctuate around 500 metres. Very strong Easterly winds will back North-East throughout the forecasting period, while steadily decreasing to become moderate.
Observed Weather Influences: Very strong Easterly winds. Light snowfall throughout the morning, drier later. The freezing level was around 550 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh deposits of unstable windslab are developing on South-West through West to North-West aspects above 900 metres. Wind-sheltered terrain such as coire rims and gully tops are most affected. Elsewhere stability is generally good with the snowpack being mostly firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Unstable windslab will be deposited throughout the period on South-West, through West to North aspects above 900 metres. North-East aspects will be subject to cross loading. Coire headwalls, crag aprons and steep slopes will be most affected. At lower elevations steep banks and burn lines may be subject to isolated deposits. Elsewhere above 1000 metres weaknesses remain present beneath a widespread friable crust. Fragile cornices will develop. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snow is expected to fall above 300 metres overnight, followed by a largely dry day. Winds will be mostly East-North-East, very strong overnight, backing to the North-East and decreasing to fresh by the end of the day. The freezing level will be between 400 and 500 metres during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Snow fell throughout the period accompanied by very strong to gale force winds from the East-South-East. The freezing level was around 700 metres at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: Unstable windslab continues to develop on West to North aspects above 900 metres. North-East aspects are also subject to cross loading. Coire headwalls, crag aprons and steep wind sheltered locations are most affected. Elsewhere, some areas are becoming wind scoured exposing very firm snow or ice. A dusting of snow is present down to around 500 metres. Fragile cornices continue to develop. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Areas of unstable windslab will be present in steep wind sheltered locations mainly on South-West through West to North aspects above 850 metres. Accumulations will be  mostly around coire rims, ridge flanks, gullies and sheltered hollows. Elsewhere the snowpack will become increasingly refrozen, icy and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snow showers this evening, above 400 metres, with strong Easterly winds. Remaining dry for the rest of the period with the winds steadily easing. The freezing level will be around 500 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Colder today with cloudy summits and showers through the day, snow falling above around 600 metres. Winds were fresh to strong from variable South-Easterly directions.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh snow accumulations are forming above around 600 metres landing on the existing snowpack which has started to refreeze and is generally firm and stable. Above around 800 metres these fresh accumulations are gaining some depth with areas of unstable soft windslab forming in steep wind sheltered locations, mainly on South-West through West to North aspects. This hazard continuing to increase during the afternoon. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.
